description: Native, leak-free macOS menu-bar script runner — the modern successor to xbar and SwiftBar. Runs any xbar/SwiftBar plugin unchanged, and adds a native AppKit/SwiftUI UI, a trust/transparency layer, a built-in plugin catalog, widgets, and typed SDKs (TypeScript, Python, Go).
rules: Vee targets macOS 26+ on Apple Silicon (arm64) only; there is no Intel build.

A plugin is any executable in any language. Vee runs it on a schedule and renders its stdout as menu-bar titles and dropdown menus, following the full xbar/SwiftBar protocol (--- / -- menus, | params, <xbar.*>/<swiftbar.*> headers, SF Symbols, ANSI, Markdown, streaming, cron).
The refresh interval comes from the filename (e.g. cpu.10s.sh runs every 10s); there is no separate interval tag.
Plugins run un-sandboxed by design. They declare what they touch via <vee.*> tags; Vee shows a plain-language trust summary before install and trust badges in the Manager, but never enforces it.
Configuration belongs to the plugin: <xbar.var> declarations become an auto-generated settings form, and secret fields are masked and stored in the macOS Keychain.
Prefer the typed SDKs (TypeScript, Python, Go) with Menu/Section builders over hand-formatting stdout when writing new plugins.
For widgets, choose the surface with <vee.surface> (menu, both, or widget) and emit a JSON widget card. The widget renders an app-produced snapshot and never executes the plugin itself.
